prestazioni: Engine swap to a 2.8L 12v VR6 (AAH engine code) with a Schrick 272° camshaft, ported and polished cylinder heads by VW Motorsport, and a custom 6-3-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold by Supersprint with a 2.5" dual-exit system terminating in a Remus Powerizer muffler for optimized scavenging and a 15-20 HP gain over stock.

Full Syncro drivetrain reinforcement with a Quaife ATB differential in the rear, a custom 4.11:1 final drive ratio from a VW Transporter Syncro, and a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch with a lightweight flywheel (12.5 lbs) to handle the increased torque from the VR6 while maintaining off-road capability.

Custom ECU remap by HJS Motorsport targeting 220 HP and 210 lb-ft of torque (from the stock 174 HP) with a focus on off-road torque delivery, including a bespoke ignition map for high-altitude operation and a secondary fuel pressure regulator by AEM for consistent performance in extreme conditions.
estetica: Custom Rallye-inspired widebody kit by Kamei (original VW Motorsport supplier) with extended fender flares in carbon-Kevlar composite, designed specifically for the Golf Country’s raised suspension and 15" BBS RM012 wheels with 215/65R15 BFGoodrich Mud-Terrain T/A KM3 tires.

Full OEM+ restoration with period-correct VW Motorsport decals, a custom roof rack by Thule (model 781) modified to fit the Country’s unique roof rails, and a front bumper lip spoiler by Zender (part #30-10-0034) painted in VW L90E Pastel White to match the original color.

Interior overhaul with Recaro SRD front seats (retrimmed in black Alcantara with red stitching), a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter) with a quick-release hub, and a custom center console by OMP housing a Stack ST8100 digital dash and a fire suppression system by Lifeline (model Zero 2000).
